A Family Adventure

Dudes at
the Fjords

Oslo · Aurland · Nærøyfjord · Hardanger · Bergen · Copenhagen

DatesJune 19–29, 2026
TravelersFer, Vic, Dolores & Juan
Days10 Days
CountriesNorway & Denmark
Weather Itinerary Flights Packing

What to Expect

Weather by Destination

Oslo
Jun 19–20
🌼
20°C / 68°F
Nights: 10°C / 50°F
Mild and dry. ~18h daylight. Light jacket for evenings.
Flåm / Aurland
Jun 20–22
🌦
14°C / 57°F
Nights: 5°C / 41°F
Fjord valley — cooler than Oslo. Rain likely. ~19h daylight. Layers essential, especially camping nights.
Hardanger / Trolltunga
Jun 23–25
⛅
15–20°C
Summit nights: ~0°C
Late June is most stable hiking season. Mountain weather changes fast. Midnight sun active. Warm layers critical for overnight.
Bergen
Jun 25–26
🌧
15°C / 59°F
Nights: 9°C / 48°F
Norway’s rainiest city. Waterproof essential. Jun 27 is statistically one of the driest days of the month.
Copenhagen
Jun 26–29
🌼
20°C / 68°F
Nights: 12°C / 54°F
Best weather of the trip for city walking. ~8h sunshine. Light rain possible.

Day by Day

Full Itinerary

01Day
Friday, June 19
Arrival in Oslo
📍 Oslo, Norway
Oslo
  • 1:00 PM
    Dolores & Juan arrive — Oslo Gardermoen (OSL)
    Taxi or airport express train to Sommerro Hotel (~20 min by train).
  • 3:00 PM
    Check in — Sommerro Hotel
    Check-in from 3:00 PM. If arriving early, luggage storage available. Easy walk through Frogner neighborhood while waiting.
  • Evening
    Fer & Vic arrive — meet at hotel
    Easy walk through Grünerlokka neighborhood together.
  • 8:30 PM
    Dinner — Eero
    Oslo’s celebrated Nordic restaurant. Confirmed reservation for 4.
    ✓ Confirmed · 4 guests
🏨
Sommerro Hotel ✓ Breakfast included
Check-in Jun 19 from 3:00 PM · Check-out Jun 20 · 2 rooms
02Day
Saturday, June 20
Oslo → Stegastein → Flåm / Aurland
📍 Oslo · Stegastein · Aurland, Norway
Drive Day
  • Morning
    Breakfast at Sommerro (included)
    Last proper hotel breakfast for a few days — take your time.
  • 9:00 AM
    Car rental pickup — Holbergsgaten 30, Oslo
    Expedia booking #73407927874472. Decline CDW (car insurance) at the counter — Chase Sapphire Preferred covers it fully. No need to purchase anything extra.
    ✓ Confirmed
  • ~5 hrs
    Drive Oslo → Aurland (~300 km)
    Take the E16 west through Lærdal Tunnel (world’s longest road tunnel, 24 km). Allow ~5 hours total with stops.
    ~300 km · ~5 hrs with stops
  • En route
    Stop — Stegastein Viewpoint
    A steel and pine platform extending 30m out from the mountainside, 650m above Aurlandsfjord. One of Norway’s most photographed landmarks — designed by architects Todd Saunders & Tommie Wilhelmsen. ~8 km from Aurland village, road is narrow with 7 hairpin turns. 20–30 min stop.
    📍 8 km from Aurland · Free · Open all year
  • 3:00 PM+
    Check in — Wangen Apartments, Aurland
    Check-in from 3:00 PM. Parking included at the property.
  • Afternoon
    Supermarket stop in Flåm
    Pick up breakfast supplies for 2 mornings. Do this in Flåm center — very limited shops in Aurland village itself.
    🛒 Don’t skip this
  • If time
    Optional: Flåmsbana train to Myrdal + bike back
    Famous mountain railway up to Myrdal (20 min), then cycle back down ~20 km. Note: bikes must be rented in Myrdal, not Flåm.
    ⚠ Bikes rented in Myrdal, not Flåm
  • Evening
    Dinner in Flåm — to be booked
    Ægir Bryggeri (Viking-style brewpub, solid food) or Flåm Bistro & Bar. Keep it easy after a long drive day.
    ⚠ No reservation yet
🏠
Wangen Apartments, Aurland
14 Vangen, 5745 Aurland · Check-in Jun 20 from 3:00 PM · Check-out Jun 22 · 2 units · Parking included
03Day
Sunday, June 21
Nærøyfjord Kayak — Day 1
📍 Flåm → Stokko Camp
Adventure
  • 9:00 AM
    Meet guide — Flåm shoreline (near train station)
    Exact GPS coordinates provided in 57hours booking confirmation packet. Leave car at public parking near Flåm train station.
    ⚠ Fer has messaged 57hours to confirm exact meeting point — awaiting reply
  • 9 AM–2 PM
    Kayak into Nærøyfjord (~4–5 hrs)
    Paddle deep into the world’s narrowest UNESCO fjord, away from cruise ship routes. All gear included: kayak, paddle, wetsuit, dry bags.
  • Afternoon
    Set up camp at Stokko — wilderness dinner
    Remote campsite. Tents, sleeping bags, and mats all provided by guides. Dinner included. The fjord under midnight sun at 11pm is extraordinary.
⛺
Stokko Wilderness Camp
All camping equipment provided · All meals included
Luggage: Confirm with 57hours whether you can leave bags at Wangen Apartments during the kayak days. If not, there are lockers at the meeting point.
04Day
Monday, June 22
Nærøyfjord Kayak — Day 2
📍 Stokko → Flåm
Adventure
  • Morning
    Wake up, pack camp, breakfast (included)
    Take time to absorb the fjord at dawn before breaking camp.
  • Morning
    Hike to Stigen farm (3 hrs)
    Climb to a historic abandoned farm with bird’s-eye views over the entire Nærøyfjord. One of the most dramatic vantage points in western Norway.
  • ~4–5 PM
    Paddle back to Flåm — return to car
    Confirm exact return logistics with 57hours.
    ⚠ Confirm return transport with 57hours
  • Evening
    Dinner — no reservation
    Casual dinner after two days on the water. Wangen has a kitchen if you prefer to cook.
🏠
Wangen Apartments, Aurland
14 Vangen, 5745 Aurland · Night 2 · Parking included
05Day
Tuesday, June 23
Aurland → Hardanger via Vøringsfossen
📍 Eidfjord · Vøringsfossen · Hotel Sundal
Drive Day
  • 10:00 AM
    Check out Wangen Apartments
    Checkout by 10:00 AM. Pack car and head east toward Hardanger (~3 hrs drive).
  • Midday
    Lunch stop — Eidfjord area
    Small fjord village on the Hardangerfjord. Good spot for a light meal. Café Solsida in Eidfjord is a solid local option with fjord views.
  • Afternoon
    Stop — Vøringsfossen waterfall
    Norway’s most famous waterfall, plunging 182m into the Måbødalen canyon. Short walk from the car park to the viewpoint — don’t skip it.
  • Afternoon
    Drive along Hardangerfjord — RV7
    One of Norway’s most beautiful drives. The orchard villages along the fjord (Ulvik, Lofthus) are in bloom in June. Stop anywhere that looks good.
  • ~6:00 PM
    Check in — Hotel Sundal
    Leave valuables here — they’ll hold them safely while you do Trolltunga.
  • 6:30 PM
    Dinner at Hotel Sundal (included with stay)
    Let them know if running slightly late — they’re flexible.
    ✓ Confirmed
🏨
Hotel Sundal, Hardanger ✓ Breakfast included
Booking #XD39GV07UM · Check-in Jun 23 · Check-out Jun 24 · 2 rooms · 8,280 NOK paid
06Day
Wednesday, June 24
Trolltunga — Day 1
📍 P3 trailhead → Summit Camp
Adventure
  • Early AM
    Breakfast at Hotel Sundal (included) — check out
    Leave valuables at the hotel — they’ll hold them until you return tomorrow afternoon.
  • ~50 min
    Drive to P3 parking (Mågelitopp)
    Recommended for the group — P3 cuts the first 4 km of steep asphalt. Must be pre-booked online, sells out months in advance.
    ⚠ Book P3 parking now — fills fast
  • Morning
    Meet 57hours guide at P3
    Confirm exact meeting time with guide. Private overnight hike. All heavy gear pre-set at basecamp — no need to carry tents.
    ⚠ Confirm guide meeting time with 57hours
  • All day
    Hike to Trolltunga cliff (~12 km from P3, 800m ascent)
    Spread across two days so pace is very manageable. Snacks and dinner provided by guide. Bring your own lunch for Day 1.
  • Evening
    Arrive summit camp — dinner included
    Hilleberg tents pre-set at basecamp. Dinner prepared by guide. Bring eye mask and earplugs for midnight sun.
⛺
Hilleberg Summit Camp — Trolltunga
~1,100m altitude · Tents pre-set · Dinner & Day 2 meals included
07Day
Thursday, June 25
Trolltunga Sunrise → Bergen
📍 Trolltunga → Bergen
Bergen
  • Sunrise
    Sunrise at Trolltunga cliff — 700m above the lake
    The whole reason you came. The cliff hangs dramatically above Lake Ringedalsvatnet. Take your time.
  • Morning
    Descend — back at P3 by ~2–3 PM
    Pick up valuables from Hotel Sundal if convenient. Then drive directly to Bergen (~3h 15 min).
    ~3h 15 min to Bergen
  • Evening
    Check in — Skostredet Hotel & Spa, Bergen
    Car official drop-off is Jun 26 at Bergen Parkinghouse — confirm overnight hotel parking.
  • 8:30 PM
    Dinner — Restaurant Moon
    Bergen’s celebrated seafood restaurant. Confirmed for 4.
    ✓ Confirmed · 4 guests
🏨
Skostredet Hotel & Spa, Bergen No breakfast
Check-in Jun 25 · Check-out Jun 26 · 2 rooms · Boutique property
08Day
Friday, June 26
Bergen → Fly to Copenhagen
📍 Bergen · Fly to Copenhagen
Travel Day
  • Morning
    Walk Bryggen wharf — go early
    Before 9am, before cruise ships dock. The UNESCO Hanseatic wooden buildings are genuinely beautiful in quiet morning light.
  • Mid-morning
    Fløyen hike (on foot, ~45 min up)
    Hike up through forested trails. Gentle compared to what you’ve just done. Panoramic views of Bergen and surrounding fjords from the top.
  • ~1:00 PM
    Lunch — fish soup by the harbor
    Søstrene Hagelin near the fish market. Bergen’s classic fish soup by the water. Cheap, casual, genuinely local.
  • Afternoon
    Wander Skostredet neighborhood
    Bergen’s most local area — wooden houses, independent coffee, no tourist crowds. Your hotel is right in it.
  • 5:00 PM
    Car drop-off — Bergen Parkinghouse Level 2
    4 Fjoesangveien, Bergen. Expedia #73407927874472. Booking shows 7:00 PM official time — aim to arrive by 5 PM to allow buffer before airport.
    ✓ Confirmed
  • 7:30 PM
    Fly Bergen (BGO) → Copenhagen (CPH)
    ~2 hr flight. Arrive ~9:00 PM. Airport is 20 min from Bergen city center. Eat before the airport — no dinner on board.
    ✓ Booked · $180/person
  • ~10 PM
    Check in — NH Copenhagen Grand Joanne
    Late arrival — confirm late check-in with hotel in advance.
🏨
NH Copenhagen Grand Joanne No breakfast
Jun 26–29 · 2 rooms · $2,274 · Free cancellation · Confirmed
09Day
Saturday, June 27
Copenhagen — Day 1
📍 Copenhagen, Denmark
Copenhagen
  • Morning
    Breakfast — find a café
    No hotel breakfast. Jægersborggade in Nørrebro is worth the short metro ride for the best independent spots in the city.
  • Mid-morning
    Nyhavn & Christianshavn
    The colorful Nyhavn canal houses are unavoidable and genuinely beautiful. Cross to Christianshavn — canal-side streets, fewer tourists, better vibe.
  • Afternoon
    Reffen Street Food — Refshaleoen
    Copenhagen’s industrial harbor island turned creative district. Large outdoor food market by the water — the local alternative to tourist dining.
  • 8:30 PM
    Dinner — Goldfinch
    Confirmed reservation for 4.
    ✓ Confirmed · 4 guests · 8:30 PM
🏨
NH Copenhagen Grand Joanne No breakfast
Night 2 of 3
10Day
Sunday, June 28
Copenhagen — Day 2
📍 Copenhagen, Denmark
Copenhagen
  • Morning
    Breakfast — café near hotel
    No hotel breakfast. Good options within walking distance of the NH Joanne.
  • Morning
    La Banchina — harbor café
    Organic waterfront café in Christianshavn. Wood-fired sauna, harbor swimming, relaxed Sunday vibe. Genuinely local — not in any guidebook.
  • Afternoon
    Glyptotek museum (optional)
    World-class art museum near Tivoli. 10,000 pieces including the largest Antique sculpture collection outside Egypt, Danish Golden Age painting, and an extraordinary 19th-century winter garden building.
  • Afternoon
    Vesterbro / Kødbyen
    Copenhagen’s Meatpacking District turned design and food quarter. Good afternoon coffee and independent shops.
  • 8:15 PM
    Dinner — Bobe
    Last dinner of the trip. Confirmed for 4.
    ✓ Confirmed · 4 guests · 8:15 PM
🏨
NH Copenhagen Grand Joanne No breakfast
Night 3 of 3 · Check-out Jun 29
11Day
Monday, June 29
Departures
📍 Copenhagen Kastrup (CPH)
Fly Home
  • Early AM
    Check out — NH Grand Joanne
    Early checkout for Dolores & Juan. Arrange with hotel to hold Fer & Vic’s bags.
  • ~7:45 AM
    Dolores & Juan head to CPH airport
    Kastrup is ~15 min by metro. Allow 2.5 hrs before departure for check-in and security.
  • 10:10 AM
    Dolores & Juan fly CPH → Paris (AF1751)
    Air France AF1751 · Terminal 2 · Seats 10A, 10C · Confirmation: AKLW6Q
    Connecting: AF174 Paris → Mexico City at 3:55 PM, arriving 7:50 PM.
    ✓ Confirmed
  • 12:45 PM
    Fer & Vic fly CPH → San Francisco (SK935)
    Scandinavian Airlines SK935 · CPH 12:45 PM → SFO 2:40 PM · 10h 55m · Confirmation: ZT5TA8
    ✓ Confirmed

Be Prepared

What to Pack

Hiking & Outdoors

  • Waterproof hiking boots (broken in before trip)
  • Waterproof jacket / hard shell
  • Fleece mid-layer
  • Down jacket (summit nights near 0°C)
  • Thermal base layer top — Uniqlo Heattech
  • Thermal base layer bottom
  • Headlamp + extra batteriesGroup bringing
  • Gloves + warm hat
  • Hiking socks — wool, 2–3 pairs
  • Eye mask + earplugs (midnight sun at camp)
  • Blister kitFer bringing
  • Snacks: granola bars, nuts, gummiesFer bringing
  • Small daypack for daily hikes
  • Umbrella (Bergen & Copenhagen both rainy)
  • Sunglasses with strap (for kayaking)
  • Sunscreen (UV high in June Norway)
  • Hiking pants × 2 (one for kayak, one for Trolltunga)
  • Quick-dry shirts × 5 total2 for kayak · 2 for Trolltunga · 1 extra

Clothing — City & Dinners

  • Smart jacket (leather or entretiempo — doubles for day and dinners)Works for all 5 nice dinners
  • Sweater × 2–3Oslo evenings, Fløyen, Bryggen, 2× Copenhagen
  • Nice blouse or dinner outfit × 4Oslo, Hotel Sundal, Bergen, Copenhagen — jacket covers the rest
  • Comfortable walking shoes (separate from hiking boots)

Essentials

  • Travel adapter — Type F (Norway & Denmark)
  • Small amount of NOK cash (Norway)
  • Small amount of DKK cash (Denmark)
  • Offline maps downloaded (Google Maps)
  • Chase Sapphire Preferred (no foreign transaction fees)
  • Personal medications
Dudes at the Fjords — June 2026 Fer · Vic · Dolores · Juan  ·  Oslo to Copenhagen  ·  June 19–29
